q: By going within. Nowhere you can go is more peaceful - more free of interruptions - than your own soul.
h:
“By going within. Nowhere you can go is more peaceful - more free of interruptions - than your own soul.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: Trying to define yourself is like trying to bite your own teeth.
h:
“Trying to define yourself is like trying to bite your own teeth.” —
Alan Watts

q: Obstacles don't block the path, they are the path.
h:
“Obstacles don't block the path, they are the path.” —
Zen Proverb

q: Every moment of happiness requires a great amount of Ignorance
h:
“Every moment of happiness requires a great amount of Ignorance” —
Honore de Balzac

q: No one can hurt you without your consent.
h:
“No one can hurt you without your consent.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: We must have strong minds, ready to accept facts as they are.
h:
“We must have strong minds, ready to accept facts as they are.” —
Harry S. Truman

q: Failure is just information and an opportunity to change your course.
h:
“Failure is just information and an opportunity to change your course.” —
Oprah Winfrey

q: Freedom itself demands discomfort.
h:
“Freedom itself demands discomfort.” —
Mark Manson

q: Remember we're all in this alone.
h:
“Remember we're all in this alone.” —
Lily Tomlin

q: The busy man is never wise and the wise man is never busy.
h:
“The busy man is never wise and the wise man is never busy.” —
Lin Yutang

q: Even the smallest person can change the course of the future.
h:
“Even the smallest person can change the course of the future.” —
J.R.R. Tolkien

q: It is what we make out of what we have, not what we are given, that separates one person from another.
h:
“It is what we make out of what we have, not what we are given, that separates one person from another.” —
Nelson Mandela

q: Money poisons you when you've got it, and starves you when you haven't.
h:
“Money poisons you when you've got it, and starves you when you haven't.” —
D. H. Lawrence

q: Start each day with a positive thought and a grateful heart.
h:
“Start each day with a positive thought and a grateful heart.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: Never give up work. Work gives you meaning and purpose and life is empty without it.
h:
“Never give up work. Work gives you meaning and purpose and life is empty without it.” —
Stephen Hawking

q: Wherever there is danger, there lurks opportunity; wherever there is opportunity, there lurks danger. The two are inseparable.
h:
“Wherever there is danger, there lurks opportunity; wherever there is opportunity, there lurks danger. The two are inseparable.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: Our anxiety does not empty tomorrow of its sorrows, but only empties today of its strengths.
h:
“Our anxiety does not empty tomorrow of its sorrows, but only empties today of its strengths.” —
Charles Spurgeon

q: Great acts are made up of small deeds.
h:
“Great acts are made up of small deeds.” —
Lao Tzu

q: The primary cause of unhappiness is never the situation but your thoughts about it.
h:
“The primary cause of unhappiness is never the situation but your thoughts about it.” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: If you don't read the newspaper, you're uninformed. If you read the newspaper, you're misinformed.
h:
“If you don't read the newspaper, you're uninformed. If you read the newspaper, you're misinformed.” —
Mark Twain

q: Still your waters.
h:
“Still your waters.” —
Josh Waitzkin

q: If you want peace, accept. If you want suffering, expect.
h:
“If you want peace, accept. If you want suffering, expect.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: Life is either a daring adventure, or it is nothing.
h:
“Life is either a daring adventure, or it is nothing.” —
Helen Keller

q: Cherish forever what makes you unique, cuz you're really a yawn if it goes.
h:
“Cherish forever what makes you unique, cuz you're really a yawn if it goes.” —
Bette Midler

q: Life and death are of supreme importance. Time swiftly passes by and opportunity is lost.
h:
“Life and death are of supreme importance. Time swiftly passes by and opportunity is lost.” —
Dogen

q: There are no secrets to success. It is the result of preparation, hard work, learning from failure.
h:
“There are no secrets to success. It is the result of preparation, hard work, learning from failure.” —
Colin Powell

q: You may be one person in this world, but to one person you may mean the world.
h:
“You may be one person in this world, but to one person you may mean the world.” —
Unknown

q: Do the difficult things while they are easy and do the great things while they are small.
h:
“Do the difficult things while they are easy and do the great things while they are small.” —
Lao Tzu

q: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it's the only thing that ever has.
h:
“Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it's the only thing that ever has.” —
Margaret Mead

q: The trouble with the rat race is that even if you win, you're still a rat.
h:
“The trouble with the rat race is that even if you win, you're still a rat.” —
Lily Tomlin

q: I think self-awareness is probably the most important thing towards being a champion.
h:
“I think self-awareness is probably the most important thing towards being a champion. ” —
Billie Jean King

q: You talk when you cease to be at peace with your thoughts.
h:
“You talk when you cease to be at peace with your thoughts.” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: The less you open your heart to others, the more your heart suffers.
h:
“The less you open your heart to others, the more your heart suffers.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: I put my heart and soul into my work, and I have lost my mind in the process.
h:
“I put my heart and soul into my work, and I have lost my mind in the process.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Everyday is a second chance.
h:
“Everyday is a second chance.” —
Unknown

q: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.
h:
“When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.” —
Lao Tzu

q: For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.
h:
“For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.” —
Aristotle

q: Your gift is something that you can do innately better than anything else.
h:
“Your gift is something that you can do innately better than anything else.” —
Steve Harvey

q: Know your gifts and share them.
h:
“Know your gifts and share them.” —
Les Brown

q: The more I want to get something done, the less I call it work.
h:
“The more I want to get something done, the less I call it work.” —
Richard Bach

q: Believe in yourself. You are braver than you think, more talented than you know, and capable of more than you imagine.
h:
“Believe in yourself. You are braver than you think, more talented than you know, and capable of more than you imagine.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: A goal is a dream with a deadline.
h:
“A goal is a dream with a deadline.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.
h:
“Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.” —
Vince Lombardi

q: Perhaps the most tragic thing about mankind is that we are all dreaming about some magical garden over the horizon, instead of enjoying the roses that are right outside today.
h:
“Perhaps the most tragic thing about mankind is that we are all dreaming about some magical garden over the horizon, instead of enjoying the roses that are right outside today.” —
Andrew Carnegie

q: I succeeded by saying what everyone else is thinking.
h:
“I succeeded by saying what everyone else is thinking.” —
Joan Rivers

q: Opportunity comes when you continually push forward.
h:
“Opportunity comes when you continually push forward.” —
Sonia Ricotti

q: Trying to define yourself is like trying to bite your own teeth.
h:
“Trying to define yourself is like trying to bite your own teeth.” —
Zen Proverb

q: You don't know who is important to you until you actually lose them.
h:
“You don't know who is important to you until you actually lose them.” —
Mahatma Gandhi

q: You have the freedom to be yourself, your true self, here and now, and nothing can stand in your way.
h:
“You have the freedom to be yourself, your true self, here and now, and nothing can stand in your way.” —
Richard Bach

q: Time flies whether you are having fun or not.
h:
“Time flies whether you are having fun or not.” —
Mary Engelbreit
